items.title: AstraZeneca's oral GLP-1 elecoglipron posts ~11.8% weight loss, advances to Phase 3
items.summary: AstraZeneca reported Phase 2b VISTA data showing daily oral elecoglipron drove roughly 10.5% weight loss at 26 weeks (about 11.8% at 36 weeks) versus ~0.6% for placebo, alongside type 2 diabetes data from the SOLSTICE trial. Results were presented at the ADA 2026 Scientific Sessions and published in The Lancet, and AstraZeneca is moving the small-molecule GLP-1 into an extensive Phase 3 programme. (Figures approximate — verify against primary sources.)
items.why: An oral GLP-1 nearing pivotal trials signals cheaper, needle-free weight-loss options that could reshape the economics of Protocol's GLP-1 medical weight-loss line.

items.title: Patients move less after starting GLP-1s, raising muscle-loss concerns (ENDO 2026)
items.summary: A retrospective analysis of NIH All of Us data (753 adults with adequate wearable data) presented at ENDO 2026 found daily steps fell from about 5,047 to 4,487 and moderate-to-vigorous activity dropped from ~27.9 to ~22.2 minutes per day after starting a GLP-1. Declines were steepest among men and people with musculoskeletal pain. (Figures approximate.)
items.why: Reinforces the case for Protocol's coaching and muscle-preserving wraparound alongside GLP-1 prescriptions, rather than medication alone.

items.title: Canada launches new preventive-health advisory committee on cancer screening
items.summary: Canada's federal government announced a new preventive health care advisory committee on 10 June to issue guidance on screening for conditions including cervical and breast cancer, after disbanding its previous task force. The committee is intended to set national screening recommendations.
items.why: Government-level screening-guidance shifts in major markets foreshadow evolving standards Protocol's screening protocols should track and benchmark against.

items.title: Longevitix launches AI 'clinical intelligence' platform for longevity & preventive clinics
items.summary: Longevitix launched a platform that unifies labs, wearables, notes and clinical history into physician-ready summaries, prioritized insights and personalized intervention plans across cardiovascular, metabolic, hormonal, thyroid and other domains. It is aimed at longevity, concierge, functional and preventive-medicine clinics.
items.why: Directly relevant to Protocol's integrated screening-plus-data model — a competing toolset for turning multi-omic screening data into clinician-ready plans.
